title: Xanthosoma nigrum
taxonomy: Xanthosoma nigrum is classified in the family Arumaceae, commonly known as the arum family, which comprises various species of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Alismatales, a group of monocotyledonous plants. The genus Xanthosoma itself is a group of tropical plants native to the Americas, characterized by their edible corms and heart-shaped leaves. Xanthosoma nigrum, specifically, is a type of taro plant known for its dark-colored corms and leaves. By its classification, Xanthosoma nigrum is a member of the plant kingdom, specifically within the monocot group.
